variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_vowels && printf 'Hello World
AEIOU
xyz

Programming is fun
' > /tmp/eval_vowels/input.txt && printf '3
5
0
0
5
' > /tmp/eval_vowels/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_vowels/vowels.py — a Python program that reads lines from stdin and for each line writes the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — both cases) on its own line to stdout. Empty lines yield 0.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_vowels && python3 vowels.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_vowels/vowels.rs — same I/O contract as the other variants (see Python/Go/C/C++ siblings). Idiomatic Rust 2021+. Will be compiled with `rustc -O vowels.rs -o vowels` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_vowels && rustc -O vowels.rs -o vowels && ./vowels < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_vowels/vowels.zig — same I/O contract as the other variants. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Will be compiled with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast vowels.zig` and tested by piping `input.txt` through stdin and checking against `expected.txt` (or via a check.py validator).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_vowels && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast vowels.zig && ./vowels < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
